project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS Ayodhya Kanda moment corpus
designPillars: 12 Ayodhya Kanda moments derived from ELGODS source citations
Cross-references Day 11 character roster + Day 12 Bala Kanda whitelist
Valmiki sarga citations preserved per moment
Voice cue IDs follow Day 13 register pattern vc_ayodhya_<verb>_<id>

moments.narrative: In the great city of Ayodhya, King Dasharatha performed the putra-kameshti yajna. From the sacred flames, divine payasam emerged. His queen Kausalya received it first, and on the ninth day of Chaitra, under the star Punarvasu, Rama was born.
moments.moralLesson: Birth of Rama from the sacred fire sacrifice

moments.narrative: Rama and his three brothers grow up together in the palace courtyard — learning archery from Vasishtha, wrestling in the gardens, and hearing stories of the gods.
moments.moralLesson: Rama is the eldest, the wisest, the most beloved of the four brothers

moments.narrative: Vishwamitra asks for Rama's help. The demons Maricha and Subahu are disrupting his yajna in the forest. Rama must accompany the sage with Lakshmana.
moments.moralLesson: Rama's first test of dharma in service to a sage

moments.narrative: Dasharatha tells Rama Kaikeyi's demands: fourteen years of forest exile. The king is bound by his own promise.
moments.moralLesson: A king's word must be kept, even when it breaks his heart

moments.narrative: Sita refuses to stay behind. Where Rama goes, she goes. The forest queen chooses the forest without hesitation.
moments.moralLesson: Sita's devotion to dharma and to Rama

moments.narrative: Lakshmana pledges to serve Rama in the forest. He cannot let his brother walk the path of exile alone.
moments.moralLesson: Brotherhood and dharma as the bond that keeps exile from becoming ruin

moments.narrative: Bharata returns to Ayodhya and learns of his father's death and Rama's exile. He rejects the throne Kaikeyi sought for him.
moments.moralLesson: Bharata's righteousness outweighs his own claim to power

moments.narrative: Kaikeyi demands the two boons Dasharatha promised her: Bharat's coronation and Rama's exile. Palace dharma turns to palace grief.
moments.moralLesson: Dharma can be corrupted by loyalty turned to personal desire

moments.narrative: Urmila stays in Ayodhya when Lakshmana walks into exile. She keeps the home fires burning for fourteen years.
moments.moralLesson: Urmila's quiet sacrifice ensures the household does not fall

moments.narrative: Guha the Nishada chief ferries Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana across the Ganga at Sringaverapura. The river marks the boundary between Ayodhya and the forest.
moments.moralLesson: Friendship across caste and kingdom at the threshold of dharma

moments.narrative: Bharata places Rama's sandals on the throne and rules as regent. He waits in Nandigrama for the fourteen years to end.
moments.moralLesson: A true king serves the rightful king even when denied the crown

moments.narrative: Lakshmana is born of Sumitra as the twin of Shatrughna. He shares a portion of Vishnu's essence with Rama.
moments.moralLesson: The twin bond of Lakshmana and Shatrughna mirrors Rama and Bharata
